tool.name: Filtering SSR
tool.description: for missing data (individuals and loci), null alleles and linked loci.
tool.command:
mkdir -p filter_output && mkdir -p summary && mkdir -p figure_output &&
Rscript '$__tool_directory__/SSR_filtering.R' --input '${input_data}' --name '${input_data.element_identifier}' --order '${filter_order}' --ind-md '${filter_ind_md.ind_md}' --ssr-md '${filter_ssr_md.ssr_md}' --low-quar '${filter_null_allele.low_quar}' --high-quar '${filter_null_allele.high_quar}' --multi '${filter_null_allele.multi}' --threshold-pval '${filter_ld.threshold_pval}'

param.name: input_data
param.label: Upload your SSR genotype file
param.help: Tabular file with individuals in rows and loci in columns. See format below.

param.name: filter_order
param.label: Select and order the filters to apply
param.help: Only the selected filters will be applied, in the exact order chosen here.
param.option: D: Linked loci (Exact test with Genepop)

param.name: ind_md
param.label: Maximum of missing data allowed per individual (MAX_MISSING_IND)

param.name: ssr_md
param.label: Maximum of missing allowed data per locus (MAX_MISSING_LOCI)

data.name: filtering_summary
data.label: Filtering: Number of SNPs and individuals

data.name: boxplot_fis
data.label: Fis distribution boxplot — null allele filter

tool.help:
Usage =====
This tool filters microsatellite (SSR) genotype data by applying up to four filters in a user-defined order. The user selects which filters to apply and the order. Each filter has its own set of parameters accessible in the corresponding section below.
**Available filters:**
1. **Remove individuals with too much missing data** — removes individuals whose proportion of missing genotypes exceeds the defined threshold. 2. **Remove loci with too much missing data** — removes SSR loci whose proportion of missing genotypes across individuals exceeds the defined threshold. 3. **Filter null alleles (paralogues)** — estimates the null allele frequency at each locus using the selected method (Brookfield 1, Brookfield 2 or Chakraborty). Loci exceeding the frequency threshold are removed, flagged or corrected. 4. **Filter linked loci (linkage disequilibrium)** — identifies pairs of loci in significant linkage disequilibrium (LD) using the selected test and multiple testing correction. One locus per linked pair is removed according to the selected criterion.
The order of filter application follows the order selected in the filter list.
Input format ============
**SSR genotype file (tab-separated):**
::
Ind Pop Loc1 Loc2 Loc3 ind1 pop1 120/124 200/202 150/150 ind2 pop1 120/120 202/204 150/152 ind3 pop2 124/126 200/200 152/152 ind4 pop2 NA 200/202 150/152
Missing genotypes should be encoded as ``NA``.
Outputs =======
- **Filtered SSR dataset** — genotype table after applying all selected filters, in the same format as the input. - **Filtering log** — step-by-step log detailing which individuals or loci were removed at each filter step. - **Filtering summary report** — tabular summary of the number of individuals and loci before and after each filter step, with retention rates.

citations.citation: @Manual{adegenet, title = {adegenet: Exploratory Analysis of Genetic and Genomic Data}, author = {Thibaut Jombart and Zhian N. Kamvar and others}, year = {2022}, note = {R package version 2.1.11}, doi = {10.32614/CRAN.package.adegenet}, url = {https://github.com/thibautjombart/adegenet}, }

@Manual{hierfstat, title = {hierfstat: Estimation and Tests of Hierarchical F-Statistics}, author = {Jerome Goudet}, year = {2005}, note = {R package version 0.5-11}, doi = {10.1111/j.1471-8286.2004.00828.x}, }

@article{brookfield1996, author = {Brookfield, J.F.Y.}, title = {A simple new method for estimating null allele frequency from heterozygote deficiency}, journal = {Molecular Ecology}, year = {1996}, volume = {5}, number = {3}, pages = {453--455}, doi = {10.1111/j.1365-294X.1996.tb00336.x}, }

@article{chakraborty1992, author = {Chakraborty, R. and De Andrade, M. and Daiger, S.P. and Budowle, B.}, title = {Apparent heterozygote deficiencies observed in DNA typing data and their implications in forensic applications}, journal = {Annals of Human Genetics}, year = {1992}, volume = {56}, pages = {45--57}, doi = {10.1111/j.1469-1809.1992.tb01128.x}, }

@Manual{dplyr, title = {dplyr: A Grammar of Data Manipulation}, author = {Hadley Wickham and Romain François and Lionel Henry and Kirill Müller}, year = {2016}, note = {R package version 1.2.0}, doi = {10.32614/CRAN.package.dplyr}, url = {https://dplyr.tidyverse.org}, }
